reasoning: The flagged README contains bad security advice but doesn't execute during installation. All executable components (PKGBUILD, scripts, service files) are safe. Manual instructions don't constitute package-level malware.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly classified the README as UNSAFE due to its dangerous sudo recommendation, though it's not executed by the package.
coverage_issues: Both models missed reviewing setup.sh inside et260b.x86_keygen_V03.run, though initial inspection suggests it's benign
false_positives: Qwen's UNSAFE verdict on README: Contains bad practice advice but isn't executed during makepkg/install
learnings: Improve prompt clarity: Emphasize that manual instructions in docs don't constitute malware unless executed by PKGBUILD/.install scripts
Prioritize executable files: Models should deprioritize READMEs unless referenced in build/install code
Handle nested archives: Audit tools should automatically inspect makeself/tar contents when found in sources
